title: Async Context, Abort Signals, Deadlines, and Cleanup · Inference Brief: Deadline Budget Allocation
summary: Request context is stored globally, promises continue after disconnect, timeouts race without cancellation, dependency budgets consume cleanup time, and rejected work becomes unhandled. Planned artifact: Add and verify the httpsts deadline budget allocation increment in request context, budget, cancellation, and cleanup ledger. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Allocate request time among queueing, dependencies, response, and cleanup with monotonic evidence and no negative or expanding child budgets.
Compose client disconnect, service deadline, dependency timeout, and shutdown with AbortSignal.timeout and AbortSignal.any while preserving the first cause.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Node and Express server Predict Deadline Budget Allocation · Theory Deadline Budget Allocation
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory team handles case 1311 in a burst intake service near its event-loop and queue limits. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to reserve cleanup time and combine client abort with one dependency budget; prove cleanup before the service deadline expires; then replace an average latency claim with p95 and event-loop saturation evidence. Browser code models pure decisions only. Node, Express, listener, TLS, PostgreSQL, proxy, worker, load, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates.

Before reading, predict which action preserves the Deadline Budget Allocation contract for the supplied data.
